g.text: Launchpad Combo Cheat Sheet for Vinx Scorza Firmware

text.tspan: Navigate, Left, Right, Up, Down, Follow, Play, Shift
Switch to Sequence / Pattern / Performer
Toggle transport / play
Global special note-toggle gesture for Note / Performer overview
In Note Mode it flips note 0 versus scale*5 on Gate layer and on Note style 1 note pages.
In Performer Overview it does the same on Note-track rows.
Launchpad input is stateful: modifiers are checked live, double-press window is 300ms
Scene column is always TRK 1..8.
The selected mode then reinterprets TOP 2..7 contextually, while TOP 8 remains Shift and TOP 1 remains Navigate.
Track LEDs also reflect mute, selected state, and playback activity depending on the current draw path.
Edit step exact behavior depends on selected track mode, layer, navigation, and note
style
Select track n
Mute track n
Navigation map TOP 2..5 still act as Left / Right / Up / Down arrows
Layer map for Note / Curve / Stochastic / Logic / Arp
Set first step on Note / Curve / Stochastic / Logic
Set last step on Note / Curve / Stochastic / Logic
Set run mode on Arp it sets arpeggiator mode instead
Follow mode for Note / Curve / Logic rest-probability pages for Stochastic / Arp
On Stochastic and Arp, rows 3-4 control restProbability2, rows 5-6 control restProbability4, rows 7-8 control
restProbability8, with row parity extending the value range.
Solo track n
Momentary fill releasing TRK or TOP 7 clears fills
Active only with no main modifiers held
In Stochastic keyboard view it toggles gate on the selected note and stores selected octave.
In Arp keyboard view it toggles gate and adds/removes that note from the arp engine unless MIDI keyboard mode is on.
Plain step editing with current layer and navigation
No keyboard overlay.
Track-specific overlays activate on selected note-related layers
Note Track + Note layer => circuit keyboard.
Stochastic + NoteVariationProbability => stochastic keyboard.
Arp + Note layer => arp keyboard.
Depend on track mode and selected layer
Gate / Slide layers often clamp horizontally to one page.
Note / Logic can expose up to 8 navigation columns.
Stochastic and Arp clamp note-related overlays to one or two navigation columns depending on layer.
Follow mode can auto-shift the Launchpad view on Note / Curve / Logic whenever PatternFollow includes LaunchPad.
On Arp, first / last step controls are ignored, but run mode still maps to arpeggiator mode.
A few layers behave specially
Curve Min / Max use a dedicated range map.
Stochastic Gate / Slide only operate on the first 12 steps.
Arp Gate toggles arp notes and can update the arp engine immediately.
Logic follows Note-like behavior for Gate / Slide / value layers.
Note and Logic Gate / Slide layers also clamp navigation horizontally.
When TOP 7 is held, some Note-track gestures write the bright accent note at scale*5
instead of editing the normal layer value.
Rows 4-5 act as semitone / tone keyboard for the selected note layer
If the chosen note exists in the current scale, selectedNote follows scale indexing;
bypass-scale indexing is tracked separately as fullSelectedNote.
Rows 1-3 edit the current 16-step page
With a keyboard note held: write that note and force gate on.
Without a held note: toggle gate.
Section offset is navigation.col * 16.
Stored as selectedOctave.
Direct 16-step section selection
Internally it calls setSection(0..3) and updates navigation through a synthetic button press.
TOP 7 + GRID on Note style 1 can still flip note 0 versus scale*5 on the current 16-step page.
Rows 4-5 choose note target for NoteVariationProbability editing
Rightmost keys on those rows bump track octave up/down; note selection tracks both scale note and fullSelectedNote.
Rows 1-2 set NoteVariationProbability for the selected note
Probability index is row*8 + col.
Octave select -4..+3
Used together with keyboard note selection.
Use Loop / Clear Loop / Reseed
Reseed is blocked if Use Loop is enabled or the sequence is empty.
Double-press on keyboard notes toggles gate and writes selected octave back into the stochastic step.
Rows 4-5 choose a note in the bypass scale for the arp note layer
Rightmost keys bump arp octave up/down.
Two upper-row pads are intentionally inactive.
In MIDI keyboard mode, pressing a note also injects it into the arp engine.
Rows 1-2 set gate probability for the selected arp note
Probability index is row*8 + col.
Octave select -4..+3
Separate from arp track octave changes.
Toggle MIDI keyboard / toggle Hold
Hold can only toggle if MIDI keyboard mode is already on.
Releasing note buttons in MIDI keyboard mode removes non-gated notes from the arp engine.
Double-press toggles gate plus add/remove note when MIDI keyboard mode is off.
Select scene-side pattern n for all tracks using the current execute type
Select track pattern by column=track and row=pattern
Mute track n
Pattern matrix navigation; TOP 2..5 remain arrows
Latch / Sync execute types
Default execute type is Immediate, unless patternChangeDefault forces Synced; in that case TOP 3 inverts back to
Immediate.
Momentary fill on track n
Release TRK or TOP 7 to clear fills.
Releasing TOP 2 commits latched requests.
Edited patterns are color-coded in the grid: Note dim yellow, Curve dim red, Stochastic / Logic stronger red.
Current and requested patterns are green variations.
Select track n
Mute track n
Performer navigation; only meaningful on the Overview layer
Select performer layer: Sequence Length or Overview
Set first step / last step / run mode
Set follow mode; GRID 17 toggles performer follow override
TOP 6 + TRK n solos track n.
TOP 7 + TRK n fills track n.
Press one pad for a one-step loop, or two pads to define first/last across all
Note / Curve / Stochastic / Logic tracks
On Performer enter, original first/last ranges are snapshotted.
When both held pads are released, those original ranges are restored automatically.
Layer 1: Overview maps GRID rows to tracks and columns to visible steps.
It toggles gates directly on Note, Stochastic (first 12 only), and Logic.
Current step is red; gates are green; special note scale*5 is brighter green.
Function LEDs echo the selected track section as a bar.
